epic.title: Reporting & compliance dashboards (Governance hub)
epic.summary: Turns the platform's data into the business intelligence the clinic relies on and the audit-ready compliance evidence that makes the moat real — consent coverage, the S4 register, lot recall, registration/retention watch, breach & complaints registers, the DAEN adverse-event prefill, plus a one-click inspection-readiness pack. Built on dedicated read-models/materialized views. Financial figures stay owner-gated.

stories.title: Reporting read-models / materialized views
stories.context: Dashboards read from dedicated read-models/materialized views fed by domain events + the audit stream; eventual consistency acceptable (ADR-0013). Build incrementally as modules land.
stories.i_want: read-models/materialized views fed by domain events and the audit stream
stories.so_that: dashboards are fast and don't hammer the transactional DB
acceptance: Read-models are populated from domain events + the audit stream.
Dashboards read from materialized views, not OLTP, and load within target on clinic data volumes.
Read-models are built incrementally per module.
Backfill/rebuild of a read-model is supported.
detail: Dashboards read from dedicated read-models / materialized views fed by domain events + the audit stream (ADR-0013), not the transactional DB. Eventual consistency is acceptable; views are built incrementally per module and support backfill/rebuild.
Keeps dashboards fast and avoids hammering OLTP — the foundation every report sits on.
ui_spec: No screen — backend projections feeding all of Reports + Governance.
A sample event flows from a write to a read-model projection (Sprint-0 DOMAIN-EVENTS).

data_model.fields: materialized views per metric (revenue, retention, utilisation, MRR, compliance)
data_model.notes: Fed by domain events + AuditEvent; rebuildable.

stories.title: Business analytics dashboards
stories.context: Revenue, retention/churn, no-shows, cancellations, conversion, at-risk, big spenders, membership MRR/churn and per-practitioner mix, date-filterable (REQ-RPT-1/2). Money figures owner-gated.
stories.i_want: dashboards for revenue, retention, no-shows, conversion, at-risk, big spenders and membership MRR/churn, filterable by date and practitioner
stories.so_that: I can run the business on real numbers
acceptance: Core dashboards match the prototype's metrics on the same date range.
Date-range presets + custom filtering; per-practitioner views.
Reward-cost vs retention surfaces (from PRD-06).
All money figures are gated behind the owner financial capability.
detail: The owner BI the clinic already relies on: revenue, retention/churn, no-shows, cancellations, conversion, at-risk, big spenders, membership MRR/churn and per-practitioner mix — date-filterable. Reward-cost vs retention surfaces (from PRD-06). All money figures are gated behind the owner financial capability.
Rebuilds the prototype's analytics on live data and fixes the Mindbody reporting gap.
ui_spec: Prototype: Reports (reports.png) — revenue trend, treatment mix, top treatments, new-vs-returning, retention, membership MRR; date-range presets + custom; per-practitioner.
Money figures hidden for non-owner roles (.fin gating).

data_model.fields: revenue, retention, churn, no_shows, cancellations, conversion, at_risk, big_spenders, mrr by date/practitioner

stories.title: Compliance dashboards & register exports
stories.context: Consent coverage, consult-before-script adherence (C1), S4 register export (C8), lot→clients recall, cooling-off adherence (C6), registration-expiry watch (C19), records-retention-due (C18), S4 stock discrepancies (C17), breach (C22) & complaints (C24) registers (REQ-RPT-3).
stories.i_want: compliance dashboards and exports covering consent, consult-before-script, the S4 register, recalls, expiry and the breach/complaints registers
stories.so_that: I can evidence compliance and act on gaps
acceptance: Consult-before-script adherence shows 100% by construction; any exception is flagged.
The S4 register exports a complete immutable record; a lot lookup returns all affected clients.
Registration-expiry watch and records-due-for-destruction lists render with their basis.
Breach and complaints registers are viewable/exportable.
detail: Compliance dashboards + register exports: consent coverage, consult-before-script adherence (shows 100% by construction; exceptions impossible to create), the S4 register export, lot->clients recall, cooling-off adherence, registration-expiry watch, records-retention-due, S4 stock discrepancies, and the breach + complaints registers.
Turns the moat's data into audit-ready evidence (the Governance hub overview).
ui_spec: Prototype: Governance -> Overview (gov-overview.png) — compliance metrics + register links; exports for the S4 register, lot-recall and the breach/complaints registers.
Registration-expiry watch + records-due lists with their basis.

stories.title: Adverse-event / DAEN prefilled submission
stories.context: Classify seriousness, route medicine vs device, produce a prefilled DAEN export/submission and flag mandatory cases (C12, ADR-0031).
stories.i_want: to generate a prefilled DAEN adverse-event report targeting the correct database
stories.so_that: reporting an adverse event is fast and correct
acceptance: An adverse event produces a prefilled DAEN report with seriousness set, targeting the correct database (medicine vs device).
Mandatory-reporting cases are flagged.
Recall execution + acknowledgement tracking lives in the hub.
Submission is export/file (electronic channel is an open option).
detail: Generate a prefilled DAEN adverse-event report with seriousness set, targeting the correct database (medicine vs device), and flag mandatory-reporting cases (C12, ADR-0031). Recall execution + acknowledgement tracking lives in the hub.
Makes reporting an adverse event fast and correct; submission is export/file (electronic channel an open option).
ui_spec: Prototype: Governance -> Adverse events & DAEN (gov-ae.png) — AE list, open a case, prefilled DAEN form routed medicine/device, mandatory-trigger flag, submit/export (openDaen/submitDaen).

data_model.fields: id, adverse_event_id, target(medicine|device), seriousness, mandatory(bool), prefilled(json), submitted_at

stories.title: Data-quality checks
stories.context: Carry over anomaly checks: active-but-unseen, completed-not-checked-in, duplicates, missing contacts, implausible dates (REQ-RPT-4).
stories.i_want: data-quality checks that flag anomalies in my records
stories.so_that: the data stays clean and trustworthy
acceptance: Checks flag active-but-unseen, completed-not-checked-in, duplicates, missing contacts and implausible dates.
Findings are listed and actionable.
Checks run on a schedule.
Findings feed the needs-attention digest.
detail: Anomaly checks carried over from the prototype: active-but-unseen, completed-not-checked-in, duplicates, missing contacts, implausible dates. Findings are listed, actionable, run on a schedule, and feed the needs-attention digest.
Keeps the data clean and trustworthy.
ui_spec: Prototype: Reports -> data-quality findings (reports.png) — a list of anomalies with a jump-to-fix; feeds the owner digest.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, check, entity_ref, detail, detected_at, resolved_at

stories.title: Owner 'needs attention' exceptions digest
stories.context: An owner digest of exceptions across the platform (REQ-RPT-5).
stories.i_want: a single 'needs attention' digest of exceptions across the clinic
stories.so_that: I can act on what matters without hunting
acceptance: Digest aggregates expiries, discrepancies, data-quality findings, failed payments and overdue follow-ups.
Each item links to its source for action.
Digest respects role/financial gating.
Available as an at-a-glance owner view.
detail: A single owner 'needs attention' digest aggregating exceptions across the clinic — expiries, discrepancies, data-quality findings, failed payments, overdue follow-ups — each linking to its source. Respects role/financial gating.
The at-a-glance 'what needs me' view for the owner.
ui_spec: Prototype: Today (dashboard.png) surfaces the owner exceptions digest; each item deep-links to its source screen.

data_model.fields: aggregates CredentialAlert + Excursion + Stocktake discrepancy + DataQualityFinding + DunningAttempt + overdue Job

stories.title: Inspection-readiness pack & governance hub
stories.context: A one-click inspection-readiness pack and the cross-case Governance hub (policies sign-off, waste manifests/IPC, DSAR + breach drill) (REQ-RPT-7, ADR-0030, REQ-SEC-8/9).
stories.i_want: a one-click pack that assembles the evidence an inspector would ask for
acceptance: The pack assembles consent coverage, the S4 register, registration/insurance status, IPC/waste logs and registers.
Policies & procedures sign-off is tracked in the hub.
DSAR (APP 12/13) and a breach drill are runnable from the hub.
Pack generation is audited.
detail: A one-click inspection-readiness pack assembling the evidence an inspector asks for (consent coverage, S4 register, registration/insurance status, IPC/waste logs, registers) and the cross-case Governance hub: policies sign-off, DSAR (APP 12/13) and a breach drill. Pack generation is audited (REQ-RPT-7, ADR-0030).
Makes the clinic always inspection-ready.
ui_spec: Prototype: Governance -> Audit pack (gov-audit.png) — buildAuditPack assembles the evidence bundle; the hub links policies sign-off, DSAR and breach drill.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, generated_at, contents[](register refs + logs + status), actor_id

stories.title: Custom report builder / external BI (placeholder)
stories.context: A custom report builder, external BI warehouse and cross-clinic benchmarking are Phase 2+. Placeholder.
stories.i_want: to build custom reports and benchmark against other clinics
acceptance: Placeholder — Phase 2+; v1 ships fixed dashboards + exports.
Captured so the read-model layer stays export-friendly.
detail: Placeholder (Phase 2+): a custom report builder, external BI warehouse and cross-clinic benchmarking. v1 ships fixed dashboards + exports; the read-model layer stays export-friendly so this can follow.
Captured to keep the architecture builder-ready.

stories.title: Practitioner scorecard
stories.context: The prototype's Reports → Scorecard view shows per-practitioner performance (revenue, retention, rebooking, utilisation, outcomes).
stories.so_that: I can coach the team and see who drives retention
acceptance: Scorecard shows per-practitioner revenue, retention/rebooking, utilisation and outcome/revision signals.
Date-filterable; financial figures are owner-gated.
Drills into the underlying clients/appointments.
Reads from the reporting read-models (PRD-08/READ-MODELS).
detail: A per-practitioner scorecard: revenue, retention/rebooking, utilisation and outcome/revision signals — date-filterable, drilling into the underlying clients/appointments. Financial figures owner-gated.
Lets the owner coach the team and see who drives retention.
ui_spec: Prototype: Reports -> Scorecard (reports.png, goRep('scorecard')) — per-practitioner metrics with drill-down.

data_model.fields: practitioner_id, revenue, retention, rebooking, utilisation, outcomes by date

stories.title: Capacity & utilisation report
stories.context: The prototype's Reports → Capacity view reports chair/room/practitioner utilisation and quiet windows (overview/trends).
stories.so_that: I can fill quiet windows and right-size rosters
acceptance: Utilisation by practitioner, room/chair and device over a date range, with trend view.
Quiet windows are highlighted (feeds waitlist/recall fill).
Reads from the reporting read-models.
Matches the prototype's capacity metrics.
detail: Capacity & utilisation reporting by practitioner, room/chair and device over a date range, with a trend view; quiet windows highlighted to feed waitlist/recall fill. Matches the prototype's capacity metrics.
Helps fill quiet windows and right-size rosters.
ui_spec: Prototype: Reports -> Capacity (reports.png, goRep('capacity'), overview/trends) — utilisation by resource with quiet-window highlights.

data_model.fields: utilisation by practitioner/room/device, quiet_windows, by date

stories.title: True-cost / margin (COGS) reporting
stories.context: The prototype adds true-cost reporting — cost of goods (units/vial cost, consumables) per treatment to show real margin, not just revenue.
stories.i_want: true-cost and margin reporting per treatment/service
stories.so_that: I price and plan on real profitability
acceptance: Cost of goods (product units/vial cost, consumables) is attributed per treatment/service.
Margin = revenue − COGS surfaces per service and per practitioner.
Feeds pricing & what-if (PRD-06/PRICING-WHATIF); figures are owner-gated.
Detailed accounting still defers to Xero (attribution, not a ledger).
detail: True-cost / margin (COGS) reporting: cost of goods (product units/vial cost, consumables) attributed per treatment/service, so margin = revenue - COGS is visible per service and practitioner. Feeds pricing & what-if (PRD-06); figures owner-gated. Detailed accounting still defers to Xero.
Shows real profitability, not just revenue.
ui_spec: Prototype: Finance (finance.png) — true-cost/margin per treatment using vial/consumable cost; owner-only.

data_model.fields: service_id, revenue, cogs(vial+consumables), margin by date
data_model.notes: Uses VIAL-RECON (PRD-04); attribution not a ledger (ADR-0027).

stories.title: Policies & procedures sign-off
stories.context: The prototype's Governance → Policies screen (signPolicy) tracks staff acknowledgement/sign-off of clinic policies and procedures, part of the Governance hub.
stories.i_want: to publish policies and track staff sign-off
stories.so_that: I can evidence that the team has read current procedures
acceptance: Policies are versioned and published to relevant roles.
Staff sign-off is recorded per policy version with date; outstanding sign-offs surface.
Sign-off status is included in the inspection-readiness pack.
Changes to a policy require re-acknowledgement.
detail: Policies & procedures sign-off: versioned policies published to relevant roles, with staff sign-off recorded per version + date; outstanding sign-offs surface and a policy change requires re-acknowledgement. Sign-off status is included in the inspection pack.
Evidences that the team has read current procedures (part of the Governance hub).
ui_spec: Prototype: Governance -> Policies (gov-policies.png) — policy list with versions + per-staff sign-off status (signPolicy); outstanding highlighted.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, name, version, body, roles[]
